Based on my review of the supplied papers only, here is the structured assessment.

```json
{
  "tf": "MSC",
  "query": "MSC TCR inhibition",
  "papers_retrieved": 10,
  "papers_relevant": 6,
  "paper_assessments": [
    {

      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "This site enhances PTPN22's inhibitory effect on TCR signaling",
      "rationale": "Concerns PTPN22 regulation of TCR signalling, not the MSC candidate regulator; off-target retrieval, not material to MSC."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC7937648",
      "retrieval_rank": 2,
      "relevant": true,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "primary activated T cells co-cultured with mesenchymal stromal cells",
      "evidence_directness": "direct causal or perturbational",
      "phenotype": "proximal TCR signalling inhibition or attenuation",
      "direction": "promotes inhibition",
      "supporting_excerpt": "activated T cells adhere to mesenchymal stromal cells, rapidly suppressing cytokine transcription and TCR signaling",
      "rationale": "MSCs suppress TCR-proximal signalling and Ca2+ flux; ICAM-1/CD43 blockade reverses it, disrupting TCR microclusters. Direct perturbational mechanism."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC12346228",
      "retrieval_rank": 3,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "review",
      "experimental_system": "human; TCR repertoire bioinformatics for adoptive therapy",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "using individual patient TCR repertoire data to select T cells for adoptive cancer immunotherapy",
      "rationale": "Review on selecting therapeutic TCRs; no MSC involvement and no TCR-inhibitory phenotype."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC7795906",
      "retrieval_rank": 4,
      "relevant": true,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "murine splenocytes/A20 cells with FasL-overexpressing MSCs (non-human)",
      "evidence_directness": "indirect mechanistic",
      "phenotype": "another inhibitory T cell phenotype",
      "direction": "promotes inhibition",
      "supporting_excerpt": "FasL-overexpressing MSC showed improved immunosuppressive properties",
      "rationale": "MSC engineering enhances general immunosuppression via FasL/apoptosis; no TCR signalling readout, so not direct TCR inhibition."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC12580868",
      "retrieval_rank": 5,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "humanized murine lung cancer model; MSC as delivery vehicle",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "TCR bispecific proteins were delivered to lung tumors using mesenchymal stem cells",
      "rationale": "MSCs serve only as delivery vehicles for cytotoxic TCR bispecifics; not a regulator of TCR inhibition."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC3854780",
      "retrieval_rank": 6,
      "relevant": true,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "human MSCs suppressing murine antigen-specific clonal CD4/CD8 T cells (cross-species)",
      "evidence_directness": "generic T cell biology",
      "phenotype": "general activation, proliferation or differentiation",
      "direction": "promotes inhibition",
      "supporting_excerpt": "MSCs altered T cell proliferation, marker expression, and cytokine production, demonstrating their immunosuppressive potential",
      "rationale": "Causal suppression of antigen-driven activation/proliferation, but readouts are general activation markers, not proximal TCR signalling."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC11885616",
      "retrieval_rank": 7,
      "relevant": false,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "iPSC-derived MSC differentiation (non-immune system)",
      "evidence_directness": "irrelevant",
      "phenotype": "unrelated",
      "direction": "unclear",
      "supporting_excerpt": "TGF-β inhibition enhances osteogenic potential and reduces premature senescence in iMSCs",
      "rationale": "Concerns MSC osteogenic differentiation; no T cells and no TCR-inhibitory phenotype."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC12126738",
      "retrieval_rank": 8,
      "relevant": true,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "human MSCs; IFN-γ-induced immunosuppression of T cells",
      "evidence_directness": "indirect mechanistic",
      "phenotype": "general activation, proliferation or differentiation",
      "direction": "promotes inhibition",
      "supporting_excerpt": "improves the immunosuppressive capacity by inhibition of T cells proliferation, induction of Treg cells",
      "rationale": "m6A pathway boosts MSC immunosuppression (PD-L1/IDO1) suppressing proliferation and inducing Tregs; no direct TCR signalling readout."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC10900800",
      "retrieval_rank": 9,
      "relevant": true,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "Mdr2-/- mice and human PSC organoids (non-human/human); MSC-derived exosomes",
      "evidence_directness": "associative",
      "phenotype": "general activation, proliferation or differentiation",
      "direction": "promotes inhibition",
      "supporting_excerpt": "reduce fibrosis by inhibiting Th17 cell differentiation",
      "rationale": "MSC exosomes inhibit Th17 differentiation via PERK/CHOP; a lineage/differentiation effect, not TCR inhibition."
    },
    {
      "paperclip_result_id": "PMC3444478",
      "retrieval_rank": 10,
      "relevant": true,
      "study_type": "primary experimental",
      "experimental_system": "T cells during Th1/Th17 polarization co-cultured with MSCs",
      "evidence_directness": "indirect mechanistic",
      "phenotype": "general activation, proliferation or differentiation",
      "direction": "promotes inhibition",
      "supporting_excerpt": "MSCs selectively suppress mature Th17 cells through cell-to-cell contact via the PD-1 pathway",
      "rationale": "MSC PD-L1 engages PD-1 to repress Th17 (neutralization confirmed), but phenotype is differentiation/effector suppression, not demonstrated TCR-linked impairment."
    }
  ],
  "overall_evidence_tier": "strong",
  "key_paperclip_result_ids": ["PMC7937648"],
  "tier_rationale": "PMC7937648 is a primary experimental study showing direct, perturbational evidence that MSCs suppress proximal TCR signalling and Ca2+ flux, with ICAM-1/CD43 blockade reversing the effect and a mechanistic readout (disrupted TCR microcluster formation). This satisfies the strong criterion of direct causal evidence altering TCR signalling attenuation with a functional/mechanistic readout. Remaining MSC papers add supportive but weaker context (general immunosuppression, proliferation, Th17 differentiation, checkpoint pathways).",
  "confidence": "moderate"
}
```

Confidence is moderate rather than high because the strong tier rests on a single primary study (PMC7937648); the other MSC papers corroborate immunosuppression but only at the level of general activation, proliferation, or differentiation rather than proximal TCR signalling.

paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns PTPN22 regulation of TCR signalling, not the MSC candidate regulator; off-target retrieval, not material to MSC.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: FasL-overexpressing MSC showed improved immunosuppressive properties
paper_assessments.rationale: MSC engineering enhances general immunosuppression via FasL/apoptosis; no TCR signalling readout, so not direct TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.rationale: MSCs serve only as delivery vehicles for cytotoxic TCR bispecifics; not a regulator of TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: MSCs altered T cell proliferation, marker expression, and cytokine production, demonstrating their immunosuppressive potential
paper_assessments.rationale: Causal suppression of antigen-driven activation/proliferation, but readouts are general activation markers, not proximal TCR signalling.

paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns MSC osteogenic differentiation; no T cells and no TCR-inhibitory phenotype.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: improves the immunosuppressive capacity by inhibition of T cells proliferation, induction of Treg cells
paper_assessments.rationale: m6A pathway boosts MSC immunosuppression (PD-L1/IDO1) suppressing proliferation and inducing Tregs; no direct TCR signalling readout.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: reduce fibrosis by inhibiting Th17 cell differentiation
paper_assessments.rationale: MSC exosomes inhibit Th17 differentiation via PERK/CHOP; a lineage/differentiation effect, not TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: T cells during Th1/Th17 polarization co-cultured with MSCs
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: MSCs selectively suppress mature Th17 cells through cell-to-cell contact via the PD-1 pathway
paper_assessments.rationale: MSC PD-L1 engages PD-1 to repress Th17 (neutralization confirmed), but phenotype is differentiation/effector suppression, not demonstrated TCR-linked impairment.

parsed.tier_rationale: PMC7937648 is a primary experimental study showing direct, perturbational evidence that MSCs suppress proximal TCR signalling and Ca2+ flux, with ICAM-1/CD43 blockade reversing the effect and a mechanistic readout (disrupted TCR microcluster formation). This satisfies the strong criterion of direct causal evidence altering TCR signalling attenuation with a functional/mechanistic readout. Remaining MSC papers add supportive but weaker context (general immunosuppression, proliferation, Th17 differentiation, checkpoint pathways).
